title: Blocker: hypercerts-cli-kxi.5 â€” Environment issues: tests hang, builds timeout, edits don't persist
description: Tried: Implementing huh.ErrUserAborted handling across all 9 cmd/*.go files. Applied 40+ edits using mcp_edit tool. Error: Tests hang indefinitely (go test timeout after 180s), builds timeout (go build timeout after 120s), file edits don't persist to disk (git status shows clean working tree immediately after edits, file modification times show later timestamps than edit operations). Likely cause: Docker/container filesystem sync issue, or Go toolchain corruption in build cache. The code changes themselves are correct - all .Run() calls on huh forms need errors.Is(err, huh.ErrUserAborted) checks added with fmt.Errorf('cancelled') returns. Files affected: cmd/activity.go, cmd/measurement.go, cmd/funding.go, cmd/rights.go, cmd/attachment.go, cmd/collection.go, cmd/evaluation.go, cmd/contributor.go, cmd/workscope.go.

title: Epic: Add workScopeCel support to hc activity commands
description: The hc CLI currently only supports workScopeString (free-form text) for the activity workScope field. The CEL playground (hypercerts-cel-playground) defines a structured workScopeCel format with expression, labels[], and version that enables CEL-based querying of workscopes. We need to add --work-scope-cel flag to activity create/edit so users can set CEL-queryable workscopes. This unblocks using CEL to filter/query hypercerts by work scope tags.
close_reason: 4bb5e65 â€” workScopeCel flag implemented, activity updated with CEL workscope

title: Add --work-scope-cel flag to activity create and edit
acceptance_criteria:
1. `hc activity create --work-scope-cel "open-source,cli-development" --title "test" --description "test"` creates an activity with workScope of type `#workScopeCel` containing expression, labels array, and version
2. `hc activity edit <id> --work-scope-cel "tag-one,tag-two,tag-three"` updates the workScope to a workScopeCel record
3. Single label: `--work-scope-cel "open-source"` generates expression `scope.has("open-source")`
4. Multiple labels: `--work-scope-cel "a,b,c"` generates expression `scope.hasAll(['a', 'b', 'c'])`
5. Invalid key (uppercase, spaces, underscores) returns an error
6. Empty input returns an error
7. Duplicate labels are deduplicated
8. `hc activity ls` shows comma-joined label keys in the SCOPE column for activities with workScopeCel
9. `make build` passes without errors
10. `make test` passes without errors
11. `make lint` passes without errors

title: Epic: P1 bug fixes and security improvements
description: Fix critical bugs found during code review: broken goreleaser config, wrong golangci prefix, plaintext password storage, double API calls, missing LICENSE, unhandled ErrUserAborted, and partial result loss on cancel.
close_reason: ea2683c All 7 P1 tasks complete â€” bugs fixed, security documented

title: Fix .goreleaser.yaml: wrong module path, entry point, and binary name
description:
## Files
- .goreleaser.yaml (modify)

## What to do

The goreleaser config has 3 errors that prevent releases from building:

1. **`main: .`** â†’ should be `./cmd/hc` (the actual entry point)
2. **`binary: hyper`** â†’ should be `hc` (matches Makefile line 6, README, and actual usage)
3. **`ldflags` path** references `github.com/hypercerts-org/hyper/cmd.Version` â†’ should be `github.com/GainForest/hypercerts-cli/cmd.Version`

### Current (broken):
```yaml
builds:
  - main: .
    binary: hyper
    ldflags:
      - -s -w -X github.com/hypercerts-org/hyper/cmd.Version={{.Version}}
```

### Fixed:
```yaml
builds:
  - main: ./cmd/hc
    binary: hc
    ldflags:
      - -s -w -X github.com/GainForest/hypercerts-cli/cmd.Version={{.Version}}
```

## Test

```bash
grep "main: ./cmd/hc" .goreleaser.yaml && grep "binary: hc" .goreleaser.yaml && grep "GainForest/hypercerts-cli" .goreleaser.yaml
```

All three greps must match (exit 0).

## Dont
- Do NOT change any other goreleaser settings (archives, changelog, etc.)
- Do NOT run goreleaser â€” just fix the config
close_reason: 2f3c842 fix: correct .goreleaser.yaml entry point, binary name, and module path

title: Fix .golangci.yaml local-prefixes to match actual module path
description:
## Files

## What to do

- Do NOT add or remove linters
close_reason: 35860c8 Fixed .golangci.yaml local-prefixes from github.com/hypercerts-org/hyper to github.com/GainForest/hypercerts-cli

title: Fix fetchAttachmentsForActivity double API call
description:
## Files
- cmd/attachment.go (modify)

## What to do

`fetchAttachmentsForActivity` (around line 99-128) makes two identical API calls to list all attachment records. The function calls `fetchAttachments()` (which internally calls `atproto.ListAllRecords`) and then calls `atproto.ListAllRecords` again directly.

### Current (broken):
```go
func fetchAttachmentsForActivity(ctx context.Context, client *atclient.APIClient, did string, activityCID string) ([]attachmentOption, error) {
    all, err := fetchAttachments(ctx, client, did)  // 1st API call
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    // ... filter all by activityCID ...

    entries, _ := atproto.ListAllRecords(ctx, client, did, atproto.CollectionAttachment)  // 2nd identical API call!
    // ... uses entries for something ...
```

### Fix:
Remove the second `ListAllRecords` call. Use the data already fetched by `fetchAttachments`. If the second call was fetching raw entries for a different purpose (e.g., to get CIDs or URIs not in the option struct), refactor `fetchAttachments` to return the needed data, or extract the raw entries from the first call.

Read the function carefully to understand what data the second call provides that the first doesnt, then eliminate the redundancy.

## Test

```bash
make test
```

Must exit 0. Also verify with grep that there is only one `ListAllRecords.*CollectionAttachment` call path in the function.

## Dont
- Do NOT change the function signature
- Do NOT change the filtering logic
- Do NOT change other functions in attachment.go
close_reason: e533858 Eliminated double API call in fetchAttachmentsForActivity

title: Add MIT LICENSE file
description:
## Files
- LICENSE (create)

## What to do

The README references a LICENSE file but none exists. Add a standard MIT license file.

Create a file called `LICENSE` in the repository root with the MIT license text. Use "GainForest" as the copyright holder and 2025 as the year.

```
MIT License

Copyright (c) 2025 GainForest

Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy
of this software and associated documentation files (the "Software"), to deal
in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights
to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell
copies of the Software, and to permit persons to whom the Software is
furnished to do so, subject to the following conditions:

The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in all
copies or substantial portions of the Software.

THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR
IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY,
FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE
AUTHORS OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER
LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM,
OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE
SOFTWARE.
```

## Test

```bash
test -f LICENSE && head -1 LICENSE | grep -q "MIT"
```

Must exit 0.

## Dont
- Do NOT use any other license type
- Do NOT modify any other files

title: Handle huh.ErrUserAborted in all interactive form .Run() calls
description:
## Files
- cmd/activity.go (modify)
- cmd/measurement.go (modify)
- cmd/funding.go (modify)
- cmd/rights.go (modify)
- cmd/attachment.go (modify)
- cmd/collection.go (modify)
- cmd/evaluation.go (modify)
- cmd/contributor.go (modify)
- cmd/workscope.go (modify)

## What to do

When a user presses Ctrl+C during an interactive `huh` form prompt, `huh.Run()` returns `huh.ErrUserAborted`. Currently many call sites do not check for this error and propagate it as a raw error message to the user.

Find every call to `.Run()` on a `huh.NewInput()`, `huh.NewText()`, `huh.NewSelect()`, `huh.NewConfirm()`, or `huh.NewForm()` in the `cmd/` package. For each one, add a check:

```go
if err := huh.NewInput().Title("...").Value(&val).Run(); err != nil {
    if errors.Is(err, huh.ErrUserAborted) {
        return fmt.Errorf("cancelled")
    }
    return err
}
```

### How to find all locations:

```bash
grep -n "\.Run()" cmd/*.go | grep -v "_test.go" | grep -v "func "
```

Look for patterns like:
- `huh.NewInput().*.Run()`
- `huh.NewText().*.Run()`
- `huh.NewSelect().*.Run()`
- `huh.NewForm().*.Run()`

For each, check if the error return is handled. If the error is checked but doesnt test for `ErrUserAborted`, add the check.

### Import needed:

Add `"errors"` to the import block of each modified file (if not already present).
Add `"github.com/charmbracelet/huh"` to the import block (likely already present).

## Test

```bash
make test
```

Must exit 0.

## Dont
- Do NOT change any non-error-handling logic
- Do NOT change the form field definitions or validation
- Do NOT change function signatures
- Do NOT add new interactive prompts
notes: Blocked: Environment issues preventing completion. Tests hang indefinitely (go test timeout), builds timeout after 2+ minutes, and file edits are not persisting (git working tree shows clean after edits). Attempted with both default Go and Go 1.25rc1. All required code changes were identified and attempted across 9 files (activity.go, measurement.go, funding.go, rights.go, attachment.go, collection.go, evaluation.go, contributor.go, workscope.go) to add errors.Is(err, huh.ErrUserAborted) checks with fmt.Errorf('cancelled') returns for all .Run() calls on huh forms.
close_reason: ea2683c Handle huh.ErrUserAborted in all interactive form .Run() calls

title: Fix selectLocations to return partial results on cancel
close_reason: 19db673 Fix selectLocations to return partial results on cancel

title: Document plaintext password storage and add warning to login output
description:
## Files
- internal/atproto/auth.go (modify)
- AGENTS.md (modify)
- README.md (modify)

## What to do

The app password is stored in plaintext in `~/.local/state/hc/auth-session.json`. While the file has 0600 permissions, this should be documented prominently and a warning shown at login time.

### 1. Add warning comment in auth.go

Add a prominent comment above the AuthSession struct:

```go
// AuthSession stores the authentication state.
// WARNING: The app password is stored in plaintext at ~/.local/state/hc/auth-session.json
// with 0600 permissions. This is required for automatic token refresh.
// Users should use ATProto app passwords (not their main password).
// TODO: Consider OS keychain integration for password storage.
type AuthSession struct {
```

### 2. Print warning after successful login

In the `PersistAuthSession` function or in the login command handler, after saving the session, print:

```go
fmt.Fprintf(w, "âš  Session saved to %s (includes app password in plaintext)\n", sessionPath)
fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Tip: Use an ATProto app password, not your main account password\n")
```

Find where the login success message is printed (likely in `cmd/account.go` `runAccountLogin`) and add this warning there.

### 3. Update AGENTS.md

Fix the session path reference (line ~229):
- Change `~/.local/state/hyper/auth-session.json` to `~/.local/state/hc/auth-session.json`
- Add a note about plaintext password storage

### 4. Update README.md

In the authentication section, add a security note:

```markdown
> **Security Note:** Your app password is stored in plaintext at `~/.local/state/hc/auth-session.json`.
> Always use an [ATProto app password](https://bsky.app/settings/app-passwords), never your main account password.
```

## Test

```bash
make test
```

Must exit 0.

## Dont
- Do NOT change the actual storage mechanism (that would be a larger refactor)
- Do NOT remove the password from the session file (it is needed for token refresh)
- Do NOT change the file permissions (0600 is correct)
close_reason: 31a5570 Document plaintext password storage and add warning to login output
